paper_title: Isolation and bioinformatic analysis of AeADF1

methods.description: Construction of homology models for AeADF1 protein using the SWISS-MODEL server to gain insight into the function of the identified plant ADF proteins.

steps.description: Constructed homology models using the SWISS-MODEL server (https://swissmodel.expasy.org/interactive).
steps.searchable_summary: use SWISS-MODEL server to construct homology models

steps.description: The intensive mode of SWISS-MODEL uses multi-template modeling for higher accuracy.
steps.searchable_summary: apply multi-template modeling for accurate homology model

methods.description: Alignment of AeADF1 with ADFs from other plant species to identify conserved domains.

steps.description: Multiple sequence alignment of AeADF1 with ADFs from other plant species (Arabidopsis thaliana, Malus domestica, Camelina sativa, Nicotiana tomentosiformis, Rosa chinensis and Capsicum annuum) indicated the presence of a conserved ADF domain.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple sequence alignment to identify conserved domains

methods.description: Determination of AeADF1 gene expression in different flower organs using semiquantitative RT-PCR and quantitative RT-PCR.

steps.description: The gene expression of AeADF1 was determined using semiquantitative RT-PCR and quantitative RT-PCR in different flower organs.
steps.searchable_summary: perform RT-PCR to determine gene expression levels

steps.description: Used whole flower cDNA as a template to clone AeADF1 by RACE-PCR.
